data.title: Codere Online Luxembourg reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Codere Online Luxembourg press release (NASDAQ:CDRO): Q1 Total revenue was €54.3 mm in Q1 2025, while net gaming revenue was €57.0 mm in the period, 8% above Q1 2024.
  • Net loss was €0.7 mm in Q1 2025

data.title: STAAR Surgical Company look to conduct $30M share buyback program
data.description:
• STAAR Surgical Company (NASDAQ:STAA) said its board has authorized a share repurchase program under which the company may repurchase up to $30 million of its outstanding common stock.
  • The firm is looking to finance the buybacks with cash on hand and cash generated from operations.
  • The company added that as of March 28, it had $222.8 million in cash, cash equivalents and investments available for sale and no outstanding debt.

data.title: Granite Construction says COO James Radich to retire
data.description:
• Granite Construction (NYSE:GVA) said its chief operating officer and executive vice president, James Radich, will retire from the roles. 
  • Radich will retire on July 4.

data.title: Baby is given world's first personalized CRISPR therapy
data.description:
A big breakthrough is being hailed in the world of gene editing, with the first custom-designed treatment using CRISPR technology (NASDAQ:CRSP). The therapy was developed for an infant named KJ Muloon, who was born with a rare genetic disorder called carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase 1 (CPS 1). The condition prevents liver cells from breaking down proteins properly, resulting in an ammonia buildup that can lead to irreversible brain damage and possibly even death.

Snapshot: CPS 1 is typically treated with conventional methods, like protein restriction, nitrogen scavengers, and drugs to reduce ammonia, but the treatment may only be partially effective. A liver transplant can also be a solution, but patients have to be at least a year old and a donor has to be found. As a result, an experimental alternative was pursued, which delivered the CRISPR base-editing therapy using lipid nanoparticles. 

A low dose was first given when KJ was only six months old, which was followed up by a higher intravenous infusion after careful monitoring. He just finished his third and final dose and seems to be responding well, with reduced blood ammonia concentrations and better tolerance to dietary protein. No serious adverse effects despite concurrent viral infections, and while it's still early to know the full extent of things, doctors were able to halve the medication he receives to reduce ammonia in his body.

Watershed moment? While CRISPR (NASDAQ:CRSP) is currently approved by the FDA to treat sickle-cell anemia and beta thalassemia, the treatments for those diseases rely on a one-size-fits-all approach. In contrast, KJ's treatment was custom-tailored to his genetic makeup and brings hope that "on-demand" therapies of the future could be designed to fit an individual patient's needs. The price tag for any gene editing therapy is still prohibitive (personalized therapy even more so), but the hope is that will change down the line as things move towards a platform-based approach. "As we get better at doing this, economies of scale will kick in, and you can expect the cost to come down orders of magnitude,” said lead researcher Dr. Kiran Musunuru of the University of Pennsylvania and the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia.

data.title: Los Angeles votes for minimum wage hike for tourism workers
data.description:
Los Angeles lawmakers have voted to approve a proposal that would make the city home to the highest minimum wage in the U.S. 

The Los Angeles City Council voted 12-3 to clear the plan to raise tourism workers' minimum pay to $30 per hour by 2028, when the city is slated to host the Olympic Games. The current minimum wage for workers at large hotels is over $18 an hour. 

The wage hike would apply to hotels with over 60 rooms and businesses at Los Angeles International Airport. The minimum wage would be gradually increased, starting with hourly pay of $22.50 in 2025, then $25 in 2026, $27.50 in 2027, and finally $30 in 2028.

The proposal also includes requirements for healthcare benefits as well as six hours of training to each hotel worker, covering safety, worker rights, and other topics.

The industry has pushed back against the proposal, saying it would pressure businesses that are already struggling with a slowdown in tourism.

"The council ignored dire warnings from top city officials and business leaders who stressed that this is not the time to pass an extreme ordinance that will kill jobs and hurt the tourism industry," the American Hotel and Lodging Association said after the vote.

"Instead, the city council elected to jeopardize economic recovery and stability by advancing policies that will lead to hotel closures and thousands of layoffs," it added. 

Since the vote on the proposal was not unanimous, a second vote will be held next week.

data.title: Dingdong Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00, revenue of $755M misses by $4.06M
data.description:
• Dingdong press release (NYSE:DDL): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00.
  • Revenue of $755M (+8.5% Y/Y) misses by $4.06M.
  • GMV for the first quarter of 2025 increased by 7.9% year over year to RMB5,960.7 million (US$821.4 million) from RMB5,525.1 million in the same quarter of 2024, positive year-on-year growth for five straight quarters.
  • Total number of orders increased by 12.1% year over year in the first quarter of 2025.

  • Guidance: The company is looking to sustain year-over-year growth in scale and achieve non-GAAP profits in the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: TPG-backed XCL gets private loan from Apollo, Partners Group - report
data.description:
XCL Education, a school owner and operator backed by private equity firm TPG (NASDAQ:TPG), has obtained a $400 million private credit loan to refinance its existing debt, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) funded a large portion of the loan, which attracted other lenders including Partners Group (OTCPK:PGPHF), Deutsche Bank AG (DB) and Nomura Holdings (NMR), the people said.

The facility has a five-year tenor.

data.title: Robust.AI taps Foxconn to expand Carter robot manufacturing capabilities
data.description:
Robust.AI Friday unveiled a strategic manufacturing partnership with Hon Hai Technology Group (Foxconn) (OTCPK:HNHPF), the world’s largest electronics manufacturing services provider, to expand production of its flagship Carter multi-function collaborative robotics platform. 

The partnership  will enable Robust.AI to rapidly fulfill increasing demand from its growing international customer base and strengthen the resilience of its supply chain and manufacturing operations.

“Foxconn is pleased to expand its manufacturing partnership with Robust.AI and help accelerate and scale the production of its innovative Carter robotics platform worldwide,” said Bob Deng, EVP at Foxconn.
